paper_title: A climbing robot with paired claws inspired by gecko locomotion
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/18_A climbing robot with paired claws inspired by gecko locomotion.pdf
sections_checked: 3. Grasping force of opposed gripping claws
3.2. Processes of grasping and claw detachment
3.3. Effect of number of spiny feet on grasping performance

equations_checked.notes: Right-claw moment balance M1-Fx1*h+Fy1*l1=0 was visually checked. It is retained through the reduced symmetric relation in E1.
Left-claw moment balance -M2+Fx2*h-Fy2*l1=0 was visually checked. It is omitted as a standalone formula because E1 retains the symmetric reduction.
Horizontal resultant Fx=Fx1-Fx2 was visually checked and retained as a condition for E1.
Vertical resultant Fy=Fy1+Fy2 was visually checked and retained through E1.
Whole-pair moment balance Fy1*2l+Fx*h-Fy*l=0 was visually checked. It is omitted as an independent formula because E1 retains the Fx=0 symmetric result.
Fy1=Fy2=Fy/2 was visually checked and retained in E1.
Fx1=Fx2=(2M1+Fy*l1)/(2h) was visually checked and retained in E1.

The printed arctangent relation was visually checked and retained in E1. The following prose says increasing M reduces theta, whereas the printed fixed-positive-sign equation increases theta; this is flagged as an unresolved sign-convention conflict.
Local force balance F+mu*N1*sin(alpha1)=N1*cos(alpha1) was visually checked. It is omitted separately because E2 retains the resulting force ratio.
Local force balance mu*N1*cos(alpha1)+N1*sin(alpha1)=W was visually checked. It is omitted separately because E2 retains the resulting force ratio.
F/W=(cos(alpha1)-mu*sin(alpha1))/(mu*cos(alpha1)+sin(alpha1)) was visually checked and retained in E2.
tan(theta1)=F/W=(1-mu*tan(alpha1))/(mu+tan(alpha1)) was visually checked. It is represented by the equivalent E2 force-ratio and angle forms.
theta1=arctan(1/mu)-alpha1 was visually checked and retained in E2.

theta2=arctan(1/mu)+alpha2 was visually checked and retained in E2. The prose first defines alpha2 as the negative of alpha1 but then discusses increasing alpha2; the signed-angle convention is flagged as uncertain.
Vertical-crawling force balance G=Fx1+Fx2+Fx3 was visually checked and retained in E3.
Vertical-crawling moment balance G*H=Fy1*2L+Fy2*L was visually checked and retained in E3.
Horizontal-crawling force balance G=Fx4+2Fx5 was visually checked and retained in E3.
Horizontal-crawling moment balance G*H=Fy4*S was visually checked and retained in E3.

tables_checked.notes: Transverse and longitudinal static limit loads of 700 g and 900 g were visually checked. The test used 100 g increments and stopped at overturn or failed attachment, so the values are system limits rather than isolated contact capacities.

figures_checked.reason: Gecko multilevel-locking photographs and conceptual opposed model are background; the retained Fig. 2 gives the quantitative force and angle definitions.
Irreplaceable paired-claw force, torque, lever-arm and local spherical-contact definitions for Eqs. (1)-(14); direct PDF crop and caption were visually verified.
Test platform and control hardware are adequately represented by the surface, speed and loading parameters.
The attachment, stable-grip, increasing-load and release stages are preserved in M3/V1 without estimating unreported exact values from the curve.
Two-to-six-foot arrangements are summarized in the parameter table and routing card; Fig. 6 is the direct performance evidence.
Direct evidence that preload and grasping force vary nonlinearly with both foot count and quartz-sand mesh; complete axes, legends and caption were visually verified.
Opposed-foot construction and load-test photographs are reducible to two rubber feet, ten 45-degree hook needles per foot and reported 400/400/200 g loads.
Robot frame topology and hardware are not needed to reproduce the retained contact and equilibrium equations.
Irreplaceable vertical/horizontal robot force directions and lever arms for Eqs. (15)-(18); direct PDF crop and caption were visually verified.
Robot snapshots demonstrate direction coverage but add no quantitative relation beyond the reported 0-180 degree range, 7.45 mm/s speed and 200 g load.

omitted_sections: General wall-climbing robot history and application background
Most biological gecko-foot morphology beyond opposed-claw inspiration
Detailed control electronics and battery description not affecting retained mechanics
Step-by-step gait narration beyond the alternating attached-frame assumption
Acknowledgments, author contributions and competing-interest declaration

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.4, Eq. (8) and following paragraph
uncertain_items.detail: With the printed positive sign, increasing M1 increases the arctangent argument and theta, but the prose states that increasing driving torque reduces theta. The actual torque direction must be re-derived from Fig. 2 before reuse.
The text calls alpha2 the negative value of alpha1 and then describes theta2 as increasing with increasing alpha2. A downstream model must define one signed angle convention and re-derive the branch.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.3-5 versus p.10-11, Fig. 2 versus Fig. 9
uncertain_items.detail: Section 2 describes Fx/Fy as horizontal/vertical tangential components, whereas Fig. 9 uses Fx along the wall to support gravity and Fy as the moment-producing normal component. Cross-section equations cannot be combined by symbol name alone.
Each foot-count/surface case was repeated five times, but the paper reports only bars without error bars or raw values. Exact values were not estimated from the plot.
The five-foot case is asymmetric while the two-, three-, four- and six-foot cases are symmetric, so foot count and arrangement are not fully separated.
The paper gives 0.425-2 mm quartz particles and mesh categories 10/18/25/35/40 but no Ra, Rq, PSD, correlation length or measured 3D topography.
The 400/400/200 g vertical/horizontal/inverted values are stated without sample count, uncertainty, loading protocol or exact failure mode.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.11, Table I and Section 4.3
uncertain_items.detail: The static test stops when the robot overturns or a foot cannot attach, and higher dynamic load is servo-limited. These outcomes do not isolate claw-contact capacity.

known_warnings: The local contact model assumes a spherical claw tip, hemispherical asperity, smooth support, 2D quasi-static equilibrium and Coulomb friction; it does not represent finite-tip contact on measured 3D brick topography.
Equation (8) has an unresolved torque-sign conflict between the printed formula and prose.
Equation (14) has an ambiguous signed convention for the lower-side contact angle.
Section 2 and Section 4 reuse Fx/Fy with different physical directions.
Figure 6 provides no raw values or uncertainty and the five-foot arrangement is asymmetric.
All reported g-valued capacities are retained as mass loads exactly as printed; they were not silently converted to forces.
The paper does not measure individual-spine contact, load sharing, travel saturation, local damage or post-failure redistribution.
Whole-robot static and dynamic limits mix attachment, overturning and servo-torque constraints.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs after excluding display-formula blocks, Markdown table rows and image-link rows.
